content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN), rooted at node 111. For each valid iii, node iii has a value aia_iai​ written on it.

An undirected simple path between any two nodes uuu and vvv is said to be vertical if u=vu=vu=v or uuu is an ancestor of vvv or vvv is an ancestor of uuu. Let's define a vertical partition of the tree as a set of vertical paths such that each node belongs to exactly one of these paths.

You are also given a sequence of NNN integers b1,b2,…,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1​,b2​,…,bN​. A vertical partition is good if, for each of its paths, we can permute the values written on the nodes in this path, and this can be done in such a way that we reach a state where for each valid iii, the value written on node iii is bib_ibi​.

The difficulty of your task is described by a parameter SSS. If S=1S=1S=1, your task is only to determine whether at least one good vertical partition exists. If S=2S=2S=2, you are required to find the number of good vertical partitions modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9+7109+7).
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
1≤ai,bi≤1061 \leq a_i, b_i \leq 10^61≤ai​,bi​≤106 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (40 points, time limit 1 seconds):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and SSS.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
The line after that contains NNN space-separated integers b1,b2,…,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1​,b2​,…,bN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer:


If S=1S=1S=1, this integer should be 111 if a good vertical partition exists or 000 if it does not exist.
If S=2S=2S=2, this integer should be the number of good vertical partitions modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9+7109+7).
